steps.title: Recognize the complex series
steps.content: The sentence lists three key years with descriptions (each containing a when-clause), already separated by a semicolon after the first item. We need to complete the second item and properly introduce the third.

steps.title: Apply the complex series punctuation rule
steps.content: In a series where items contain internal commas, a semicolon precedes 'and' before the final item. Option A uses 'released; and', properly connecting the second item to the third while preserving the list structure. The other options either omit the required semicolon, weaken it to a comma, or create an unwarranted sentence boundary. That matches choice A — A is correct.
